title: The mysterious three years ago incident
display_title: The mysterious three years ago incident
wikitext:
[]: The mysterious "three years ago"-incident             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Apr 05, 2012 9:47 am ...And I don't mean anything Haruhi-related. (Sorry, couldn't resist.) As early as the first episode, Fuyutsuki mentions that its the first time in three years that Shinji and Gendo see each other - three years, not ten years.(when he first gave Shinji away) The strange incident gets further mentions in episode 15, where we learn that it happened during their annual visit to Yui's grave (which seems to have been some little Ritual between the two) and led to those meetings being discontinued until 2015. And then, there are those lines from ep 16 - "But it was you who ran away", directly followed by a montage of text refering to the circumstances of Yui's death/ all that deal with Gendo being accused of having killed her. So maybe it all could've been related to little Shinji overhearing someone talk about that (Then again, he must've been 11 or something, just how far would he understand that?) and refusing to see Gendo/ confront him about it, which Gendo absolutely couldn't deal with, thus leading to him avoiding Shinji like the plague for the next three years. Or it could just be that Shinji simply hit the age where kids begin to question the actions of their parents and, asides from just being sad about having been left alone, reacted with anger and went all passive-agressive on Gendo, which again, he couldn't deal with, leading to him never showing his face to Shinji again... in any case, there must've been some mayor drama since it apparently ended with little Shinj running away in some sort. Even tough they are the main characters, there is such a glaring mystery in a relevant part of their shared backstory, and I'm a bit surprised by the lack of speculation about it since its a rather mayor blank spot compared to other things that get the hell speculated out of them. In any case, the existence of such an incident and the way it's treated as some mayor breaking point would suggest that things were notably different before, i.e., Gendo might've visited Shinji on a somewhat regular basis, sporadically enough for Shinji to turn out the way he did, but he didn't completely break off all contact before that one, myterious incident... I'd like to hear your thoughts on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 05, 2012 12:27 pm I'm not sure there's any incident to speak of. That was just the last time they saw one another; nothing more to it than that. What I find more interesting is the fact that Ritsuko also last visited her mother's grave three years ago. Is the coincidence significant? Dunno. After all, no one says the two events occurred on the same day. But without more info from the show there's no basis for speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Apr 05, 2012 3:16 pm I don't get the impression that the visit to Yui's grave that we actually see is significantly different from Shinji's and Gendo's previous visits (minus the helicopter with Rei in it). Gendo is just being a stoic, taciturn presence at the grave, with perhaps a few words being exchanged between father and son. I also believe they never met more than once per year. (No, not even on Shinji's birthday.) Just enough to remind Shinji who his father is, but not enough for either of them to actually get to know the other. What probably happened that they stopped doing it was Shinji questioning why Gendo left him after Yui died. Gendo, having no answer and facing his hedgehog's dilemma probably cut off the contact after that, using work as an excuse.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Apr 05, 2012 3:23 pm Bagheera wrote: I'm not sure there's any incident to speak of. That was just the last time they saw one another; nothing more to it than that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Apr 05, 2012 3:28 pm I'm reminded of the shot we see multiple times of Shinji, as a younger boy, crying as Gendo is walking away with his back turned. Shinji blames Gendo for something which I don't think is ever truly defined. We know that Shinji had ran away and began living with his teacher; Gendo, during his final scene, admits to pushing Shinji away. While this is pure speculation, and the real answer will probably never be discovered, what I think may have happened involves Shinji throwing one of his usual fits and Gendo, in anger, saying something along the lines of "LEAVE," and walking away. Shinji, being Shinji, listens and leaves for three years until he's called back.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 05, 2012 7:20 pm Sachi wrote: Shinji blames Gendo for something which I don't think is ever truly defined.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Apr 05, 2012 9:19 pm Perhaps? I'm sure Shinji accuses Gendou of killing Yui outright in one of his head-trips (during the enemy-enemy-enemy scene in 16, maybe?). Who knows how long he's believed this? Shit, without Yui or Fuyutsuki to reveal the true intent behind Unit 01's contact experiment, it is pretty reasonable to blame Gendou for Yui's death - to let your head researcher disintegrate herself during a test you personally supervise is borderline negligent homicide. Combined with the fact that the guy is a complete dick to him, Shinji could have begun to believe that Gendou had gotten Yui killed at a pretty early date.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 05, 2012 9:35 pm Azathoth: Yeah, I did seem to recall Shinji directly accusing him somewhere, but I had a numero uno brain fart and forgot to scan the episode 20 transcript for the actual quote. :hangs head in shame: Episode 20 wrote: Shinji: Damn! Damn! Damn! How dare you hurt Toji and kill my mother! Father!

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Fri Apr 06, 2012 3:41 am @ Sachi - Would it be better to think that the strongest grudge that Shinji might bear towards Gendo is 'undefined' only in that he's being blamed for not filling a place in Shinji's life? From there, perceiving all sorts of other bad deeds, such as replacing him with Rei or separating him from Yui are just par for the course. That puts me on the same track as Jornophelanthas . Then perhaps the event that changed their pattern three years ago was a product of Shinji's developing intellect mashing up against Gendo's defensive stance. Essentially, this may have been meant to represent the first time Shinji was able to articulate any of his thoughts in a way that Gendo would rather not tolerate. Perhaps the timing is the 'dawn of the age of Reason' for Shinji. Asking the wrong loaded question is more than sufficient to kick off Gendo's harsh distancing techniques, most likely. There are several 'hot button' issues to choose from, as well. It would lead to some manner of reply that sent Shinji even deeper into anger. It's the best I can piece together, given the very circumspect way it is given to us. Is the lack of a direct view for this history meant to represent to the audience that Shinji, and to a lesser extent Gendo, didn't truly understand their exchange at that time, only the resulting trauma?

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Apr 06, 2012 5:16 am Azathoth wrote: Shit, without Yui or Fuyutsuki to reveal the true intent behind Unit 01's contact experiment, it is pretty reasonable to blame Gendou for Yui's death - to let your head researcher disintegrate herself during a test you personally supervise is borderline negligent homicide. Combined with the fact that the guy is a complete dick to him, Shinji could have begun to believe that Gendou had gotten Yui killed at a pretty early date.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Apr 06, 2012 5:23 am Jornophelanthas wrote: I don't recall Shinji ever being told about Yui's contact experiment

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 06, 2012 5:37 am Jornophelanthas wrote: I don't recall Shinji ever being told about Yui's contact experiment (because that would require clearance to know about Evangelion creation and the existence of Lilith). Your entire line of reasoning is therefore unavailable to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Apr 06, 2012 6:01 am Mr. Tines wrote: He was just there to see it happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 06, 2012 6:24 am Jornophelanthas wrote: He was three, hardly able to grasp what was going on.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Apr 06, 2012 8:05 am Bagheera wrote: I don't remember what my father did when I was 2, but I know it affected me significantly at the time and has shaped the course of my life ever since. Kids know shit.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Apr 06, 2012 2:41 pm Shinji watched Mommy go inside the big fucking robot and then not come out and then everyone started freaking out like she died. It's honestly a psychological miracle he didn't have a goddamn PTSD flashback about seeing the Robot that Ate Mommy in the first episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Apr 06, 2012 3:13 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Shinji watched Mommy go inside the big fucking robot and then not come out and then everyone started freaking out like she died. It's honestly a psychological miracle he didn't have a goddamn PTSD flashback about seeing the Robot that Ate Mommy in the first episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 06, 2012 3:58 pm He *does* react strongly when Ritsuko mentions that he was watching when his mother died/that they're standing in the very room it happened, tough, to the point where Misato interferes and tells her to stop it: [URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/198/traumatizedshinchan.png/] [/URL] Ritsuko, however, seems to take quite the delight in breaking the mind of a depressed 14-yeard old: [URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/62/mindrapetsuko.png/] [/URL] Makes you wonder what she would have done with him if Misato hadn't been with them. I say there's a good chance that he would have ended up strangled. Then again, that (Shinji's reaction) was after he recalled the incident in ep 20, probably, he kept what little he knew of the incident repressed until then, and thus wasn't able to tell that it's the same giant robot when he first saw it. ...Kinda harsh. The only thing he remembers about his mom is the way she died.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Mon Apr 09, 2012 7:39 pm Gendo's "But it was you who ran away" speech might have just been to guilt-trip his son into bonding with his mother more (which was an integral part of his plan). Him being an asshole to his son is nothing new.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Apr 10, 2012 1:56 pm Kendrix wrote: I say there's a good chance that he would have ended up strangled.
